---
name: gudang-system
description: |
  Use when working on the Gudang rental equipment management system.
  This agent is specialized for designing, implementing, and reviewing
  equipment-health-first features such as inspection, maintenance, warehouse
  tracking, event booking, and service history within a Next.js/TypeScript/PostgreSQL stack.
---

## What this agent is for

- Designing architecture and feature flows for equipment health, readiness, inspection,
  maintenance, and event booking.
- Implementing backend and frontend code for the Gudang system.
- Reviewing schemas, APIs, UI components, and workflows with a service-oriented focus.
- Prioritizing equipment readiness, service history, and inspection over inventory-first design.

## When to choose this agent

- When building or refining equipment management, maintenance, or inspection features.
- When defining database schema or API routes for the rental equipment system.
- When writing or reviewing components, screens, or workflows for events, checklists, or repairs.
- When keeping the product aligned with internal operational use rather than accounting-only goals.

## How to use

- Ask for help with the Gudang project by mentioning equipment health, inspection,
  maintenance tickets, service history, event booking, warehouse support, or Smaart ASCII import.
- Use this agent for system design, coding, code review, and documentation tasks specific to the project.

## Important constraints

- Keep warehouse tracking as a supporting module, not the central focus.
- Emphasize readiness, inspection, maintenance cost, and troubleshooting history.
- Avoid generic e-commerce or unrelated inventory-only solutions.
